plugins.name: dotnet-coding-conventions
plugins.description: Teaches Claude idiomatic C# conventions—null guards, collection initializers, logging, braces, using directives. The nits you'd get a brain splinter fixing in code review.

plugins.name: documenting-csharp-code
plugins.description: Keeps Claude from writing verbose XML docs. Enforces concise, one-sentence summaries focused on contract over implementation.

plugins.name: testing-xunit-effectively
plugins.description: Stops Claude from writing ten identical tests. Enforces behavior-driven testing with [Theory] to eliminate duplication.

plugins.name: git-conventional-commits
plugins.description: Enforces Conventional Commits spec. Makes commit messages explain intent, not just list files.

plugins.name: dotnet-ilspy
plugins.description: Teaches Claude to dnx peek at .NET source code instead of just web searching. See how the framework actually works inside.

plugins.name: docs-mailchimp-voice
plugins.description: Rewrites robotic documentation to sound human. Clear, confident, helpful tone—like a teammate, not a textbook.

plugins.name: dotnet-nswag
plugins.description: Teaches Claude how to generate API clients from OpenAPI specs using dotnet dnx or nswag CLI. All the commands and flags.
